blocks.text: Cindergrove Advisors Inc
Ms. Heather King, VP Finance, Wagner-Greene
Re: Plant Consolidation Support
Dear Ms. King:

Thank you for the courtesy shown to our firm during our recent visits. This letter confirms the terms under which Cindergrove Advisors Inc will assist {{fact:f:E-2003-001.client}} with the consolidation of its plant operations. I would ask that you review it and return a countersigned copy by fax at your convenience.

Our work will cover the operations of both Wagner-Greene plants. We will review production scheduling, inventory positions, freight arrangements, and staffing at each location, and we will develop a consolidation plan setting out the sequence of moves, the workforce implications, and the expected effect on cost and service. Our practice is to present findings before recommendations, and to support every recommendation with figures drawn from your own records rather than from industry generalities. We expect the work to run approximately four months from the start of fieldwork to the delivery of the final plan.

Kimberly Price, Senior Consultant, will direct the engagement and will be your principal point of contact throughout. Andrea Diaz, Consultant, will carry out the on-site observation and data collection at both plants. I will review all written work before it is issued under the firm's letterhead and will attend the steering meetings personally.

Our fee for this engagement will be {{fact:f:E-2003-001.fee}}, covering all professional time. Travel and out-of-pocket expenses will be billed as incurred, at cost and without markup, with receipts furnished on request. Subject to your countersignature, we propose to begin fieldwork on {{fact:f:E-2003-001.start}}.

We appreciate the confidence Wagner-Greene has placed in our firm, and we will conduct this work with the care it deserves. If any term of this letter does not reflect your understanding, please telephone me before signing. Very truly yours,
Meeting Minutes: Plant Consolidation Support

The engagement team met with representatives of {{fact:f:E-2003-001.client}} to review progress on the plant consolidation study. These minutes were prepared by Andrea Diaz and are circulated to all attendees for correction before they are placed in the engagement file.
Attendees

items: Kimberly Price, Senior Consultant, Cindergrove Advisors Inc
Andrea Diaz, Consultant, Cindergrove Advisors Inc

blocks.text: Discussion

Kimberly Price reviewed the field observations completed to date at the first plant, covering production scheduling and inventory handling. She noted that the observations support the working view that scheduling practices differ materially between the two locations, and she cautioned against drawing conclusions until the second plant has been walked in the same manner.

Heather King asked how the analysis would treat freight costs. Andrea Diaz reported that the freight cost detail has not yet been received from Wagner-Greene; rather than estimate, the team has recorded this as an open item below. Ms. King agreed to have the detail pulled by her accounting staff and sent to the engagement team.
Action Items

rows: Provide freight cost detail to the engagement team
Complete floor observations at the second plant
Prepare preliminary consolidation findings for internal review

blocks.text: Status Report: Plant Consolidation Support
Submitted to {{fact:f:E-2003-001.client}} by Kimberly Price, Senior Consultant, Cindergrove Advisors Inc.
Status Summary

The engagement is on schedule and within the agreed fee of {{fact:f:E-2003-001.fee}}. Fieldwork at both plants is complete, the freight cost detail has been received and analyzed, and drafting of the consolidation plan has begun. Findings continue to be developed ahead of recommendations, consistent with our practice, and we see no obstacle to completing the work within the period set out in the engagement letter.
Accomplishments This Period

items: Andrea Diaz completed floor observations at the second plant, closing out the fieldwork phase of the engagement.
Freight cost detail was received from Wagner-Greene accounting and reconciled against the shipment records gathered during fieldwork.
Preliminary findings on scheduling and inventory handling were drafted and reviewed internally before circulation.

blocks.text: Upcoming Work

The team will complete the consolidation plan, including the sequence of moves and the staffing implications at each location, and will prepare a steering-committee presentation of findings and recommendations. We will review the draft plan with Wagner-Greene management before the presentation is finalized, so that no figure or finding comes as a surprise in the meeting.
Risks

Two matters deserve attention. First, the freight analysis rests on current carrier rates; if those rates are renegotiated during the consolidation, the savings estimates will need to be revised accordingly. Second, the timing of workforce communication remains open. We recommend that this be settled before the consolidation plan is presented, as the fieldwork indicated that plant staff are already aware a study is under way.
